name: Oracle
philosophy: Oracle's web presence is corporate-database institutional — bright red (#c74634) as the signature chromatic asset on the wordmark and primary CTAs, a clean white canvas, and a Roboto-family humanist sans across the chrome. The atmosphere is enterprise-formal — minimal whimsy, dense product taxonomy (database, cloud, ERP, HCM), and a layout grammar engineered for IT decision-makers rather than end-users.

design_language.motion_signature: Minimal motion vocabulary — slight 150ms ease on hover, no scroll-triggered reveals on enterprise product pages. The brand reads as serious documentation, not marketing performance.

trademark_signals: Oracle Red (#c74634) — the signature chromatic asset, used on the wordmark and primary CTAs. A specific saturated tomato-red, not a pure red
Oracle Sans / Roboto-family humanist sans — the chrome typography
Dense product taxonomy navigation — multi-level mega-menus covering Cloud, Database, Applications, Industries, Support
Rectangular product cards with 0–4px radius — the brand reads as documentation-precise rather than soft
Documentation-style chrome — headers, breadcrumbs, sidebar TOCs across most product pages
ai_slop_avoided: Don't soften the rectilinear card radii — Oracle reads as documentation, not consumer SaaS
Don't replace Oracle Red with a 'warmer' coral — the specific brick-red is part of corporate recognition
Don't add character illustrations or playful mascots — Oracle's brand voice is enterprise-formal
Don't reduce the mega-menu density — the multi-level taxonomy is part of the brand's enterprise IA
